lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Hello again. How is your quest going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't actually started yet.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Well you need to find out more about that expedition. The Library Historical Archive should have records on it. Talk to Archeio in the Arceuus Library. He can take you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Hello there. Not very often I get visitors. What brings you here?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: The name's Phileas Rimor. I was once a soldier in the Shayzien Army but now I'm retired.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you need help with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you need help with anything?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: I'm fine thank you. Thanks for asking though.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Well let me think...

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: You know, there is something I wouldn't mind some help with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! What is it?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: When I was a boy, my grandfather would tell me the story of my ancestor, Magnus Rimor. He was a great explorer who lived a thousand years ago.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: One day, Magnus was approached by none other than King Shayzien VII himself. The King asked him to lead an expedition to explore Mount Quidamortem in the Kebos Lowlands.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Alas, according to records, the party never returned and were declared to have perished in the field.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's very interesting and all but why are you telling me this?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: A few days ago, I received a parcel. There was no name on it and I never saw the person that delivered it. Inside, I found something truly fascinating.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: There was an old journal, along with a note. The note claims that the journal was stolen from the Tower of Magic during the uprising against Xeric.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: But that's not all. Apparently the journal was written by King Shayzien VII himself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Again, really great and all but I still don't see where I come into this.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Most of the journal is illegible but I've still managed to find a few readable extracts. Listen to this one...

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: 'Quidamortem's creatures are compatible. The experimentation is proving successful and we will soon be ready to mass produce.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: That extract had a date in the 37th decade but the expedition was declared a failure in the 36th decade! If the expedition truly was a failure, why was the King writing about Quidamortem years later?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe there was another expedition.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Maybe. Or maybe someone wasn't telling the truth about that expedition. Regardless, I must find out what really happened. Can you help me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: We need to find out more about that expedition. The Library Historical Archive should have records on it. Talk to Archeio in the Arceuus Library. He can take you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't sound too hard. Leave it with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got other things to do.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Oh, okay then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Archeio
lines.text: Hello. Would you like to visit the Library Historical Archive? We've recently opened the archive to visitors.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: Welcome to the Library Historical Archive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question about King Shayzien VII.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: Well you've come to the right place. What would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm searching for information on an expedition to Mount Quidamortem during his reign.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: Ah yes, I know of that expedition. King Shayzien VII was one of Kourend's most notable rulers. According to the history books, his reign was defined by violence.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: The expedition to Quidamortem was part of his aggressive plans to expand Kourend's territory. Unfortunately, it was a failure. The entire party was killed.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: The King himself died a few years later and was succeeded by Xeric, a tyrannical mage who made the King seem meek in comparison. Though of course, many believe them to actually be the same person.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: But I digress. The key thing to note is that no more expeditions were ordered under King Shayzien VII's rule. Does that help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not really. I have reason to believe that not everyone was killed on that expedition.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't help you there. All the evidence we have tells us that the expedition had no survivors.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I'm at a dead end?

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: It would seem that way. However, if you do want to learn more about King Shayzien VII, it might be worth talking to Pagida. He's recently been investigating the King's additions to the Tower of Magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Pagida
lines.text: Hello there, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question about King Shayzien VII.

lines.speaker: Pagida
lines.text: What do you wish to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to find out more about the expedition he ordered to Mount Quidamortem.

lines.speaker: Pagida
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have any information on that expedition. You're probably better off talking to one of the others.

lines.speaker: Pagida
lines.text: I could tell you about the King's work on the Tower of Magic though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it's not like I have anything better to do. Fire away.

lines.speaker: Pagida
lines.text: During the time of King Shayzien VII, the Tower of Magic was the seat of power in Kourend. The King spent a lot of his time at the Tower, more than any other ruler.

lines.speaker: Pagida
lines.text: In fact, he oversaw the construction of various new sections, most notably the prison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When was this?

lines.speaker: Pagida
lines.text: Well now that I think about it, it was around the same time as the expedition you were asking about. That said, I doubt the two are connected.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Would it be possible to visit the prison?

lines.speaker: Pagida
lines.text: Well the prison is abandoned and has been for many years, so I suppose there's no harm in letting you see it.

lines.speaker: Pagida
lines.text: Be aware that the magic in that part of the tower is a bit unstable, so any items left in the prison will be lost. Would you like me to teleport you there now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Pagida
lines.text: Very well, prepare yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Pagida
lines.text: I'll be here if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. I should tell Phileas about this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Hello again. How is your quest going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need to talk to Lord Shayzien.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Well you'll find him in the War Tent in the Shayzien Encampment to the north.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Hello again. How is your quest going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I went to the Library Historical Archive. I found out that King Shayzien VII ordered the construction of a prison there around the time the expedition was taking place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I investigated the prison and in one of the cells I found a strange message.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: What did it say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Lizards on the mountain. Do not trust the King. The Altar is a lie.'

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Lizards on the mountain? So it was true! Someone did return from that expedition! Do you think it was Magnus?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Impossible to say I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: That's a shame. I would have liked to have known what happened to him.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: That's one to dwell on later though. For now, we need to work out our next steps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Next steps?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Absolutely. We now know for sure that someone returned from that expedition, but we still don't really know why. We need to find out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why does it matter? This all happened a thousand years ago. Anyone involved is long dead now.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: That doesn't mean it doesn't matter! This isn't just the chance to discover the truth about my ancestor. We also have the chance to expose the treachery of a former king!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I see you feel strongly about this. What do you need me to do?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: If we're to find out what happened on that expedition, we're going to have to retrace its route. And by we, I mean you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I had a feeling that would be the case.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: First things first, you'll need to talk to Lord Shayzien. He's ordered that any research work done on the mountain be approved by him first. You'll find him in the War Tent in the Shayzien Encampment to the

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: north.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I'll be on my way then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: How's your expedition citizen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't started yet.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Well if you require assistance, I'd recommend talking to Historian Duffy on Quidamortem. He's been researching the mountain for a while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: What do you need citizen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to perform some research on Mount Quidamortem, but I need your permission to do so.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Quite right. Quidamortem is a dangerous place and I don't want to be ordering my troops on any unnecessary rescue missions.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: So, tell me about your research.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Many years ago there was an expedition to Quidamortem ordered by King Shayzien VII. The expedition supposedly ended in failure.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: But?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I have reason to believe that wasn't the case. I think something was discovered on that expedition. I want to find out what it was.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Fair enough. I will approve your research, on one condition.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: If you discover anything, you will bring it to me first. King Shayzien VII was my ancestor and I want to be made aware of any discoveries that would present him in a bad light.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But surely the library should be made aware of any important discoveries?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: They will be made aware. But only after I have reviewed the discoveries first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Excellent. Consider your research approved. If you require assistance with your work, I'd recommend talking to Historian Duffy on Quidamortem. He's been researching the mountain for a while.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Good luck citizen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Are you Duffy?

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: That's me. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lord Shayzien told me that you might be able to help with some research I'm doing.

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: I'm rather busy with my own research.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please. It's important.

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: Well what is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to retrace the steps of an expedition ordered by King Shayzien VII. I believe something important was found on that expedition, but I don't know what.

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: Interesting. So you don't know anything about what they found?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a quote which I believe is from someone on the expedition.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Lizards on the mountain. Do not trust the King. The Altar is a lie.'

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: Fascinating. We've been researching the lizard creatures here for a while now. We have reason to believe they are not of natural origin and may in fact have been created by Xeric or his followers.

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: But this quote of yours suggests the lizardmen may have existed during the time of King Shayzien VII, before Xeric's rule.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So will you help me?

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: Yes. This is definitely worth pursuing. In fact, I think I know where we should begin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: We recently found a small crevice on the western face of the mountain. It seems to be manmade and appears to be older than the rest of the caves around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll start there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This must be the crevice that Duffy mentioned. I'll need a rope to get in.

sections.title: Using a rope on the crevice

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Rickard! Turn away! Don't do this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello? Is anyone there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, I wonder what that was about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh well, I may as well go and tell Historian Duffy what I've found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: Find anything in that cave?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: Well let us know when you do. It's on the western face of the mountain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: Hello. Did you find anything in that cave?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did. There was a weird looking lizardman along with a strange altar.

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: A lizardman? Is it safe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I took care of it.

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: Well in that case, we'll go take a look.

sections.title: Talking to Duffy in the cave

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: This cave is quite fascinating. We could really be on to something big here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What have you discovered?

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: We've never come across lizards like the ones in this cave before. It would appear that they are a previously undiscovered species.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting.

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: That's nothing. The really interesting part is that these lizards appear to be directly related to the lizardmen that roam this region.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this is where the lizardmen came from?

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: It would appear so. That's not all though. This cave definitely predates Xeric, who we originally believed to be the creator of the lizardmen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So who did create them?

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: Impossible to say for sure, but with what we've already learnt about King Shayzien VII, I do wonder if he was the one responsible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if King Shayzien VII did create the lizardmen, why did they fight for Xeric?

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: There are some who believe that King Shayzien VII and Xeric were one and the same. They claim that after his death, the King was resurrected by his followers, taking on the name Xeric.

lines.speaker: Historian Duffy
lines.text: Anyway, I'm going to carry on with my research. I'd recommend you talk to Gnosi. She's made some interesting discoveries of her own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Duffy said you'd found something interesting.

lines.speaker: Gnosi
lines.text: The Dark Altar was the key to Xeric's power. Because of it, he was able to rule Kourend unopposed for nearly a hundred years. His reign was so dark, it became known as the Age of Strife.

lines.speaker: Gnosi
lines.text: Once he was finally defeated, Xeric came to Quidamortem and built the Chambers of Xeric deep below the mountain.

lines.speaker: Gnosi
lines.text: For many years we've tried to work out how he managed to survive out here without the power of the Dark Altar. Thanks to this cave, I think we've found the answer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Gnosi
lines.text: This altar, it seems to give off the same power as the Dark Altar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this altar was how Xeric kept his power after he was defeated?

lines.speaker: Gnosi
lines.text: I believe so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why is it here?

lines.speaker: Gnosi
lines.text: It appears to be manmade. I believe this altar was created to amplify the power of the Dark Altar, allowing it to reach areas it previously couldn't.

lines.speaker: Gnosi
lines.text: This discovery does bring about an intriguing question though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Gnosi
lines.text: The altar here is still giving out power. That means Xeric could have potentially used it to live for hundreds of years after his defeat. It's long been believed that Xeric eventually died here, but what if he didn't?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a worrying thought.

lines.speaker: Gnosi
lines.text: Indeed. I'm going to stay here and continue working. Do you mind heading to the library to let them know what we've discovered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, Lord Shayzien asked me to go to him first with any discoveries.

lines.speaker: Gnosi
lines.text: Hmm, I can see why he would take an interest. Very well, go let him know what we've found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do, see you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Do you have the parcel citizen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Well go and fetch it then. I need to see it for myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: How is your research going citizen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We've made some important discoveries.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We've discovered a small cave on the western face of Quidamortem. It's manmade and it predates the time of Xeric.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Inside, we found an altar. It seems to be giving off the same power as the Dark Altar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We also discovered a unique lizard species, one that shares many similarities with the lizardmen.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: What does that mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems that this cave was the origin of the lizardmen. The fact that it predates Xeric suggests the lizardmen do as well.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: You believe they were the creation of my ancestor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hard to say for sure. It would make sense though.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: From the evidence we have, I believe that King Shayzien VII ordered an expedition to Quidamortem, hoping to discover a suitable species for his lizardman project.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Once the expedition found one, he had them killed to keep the discovery a secret.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: But we don't have proof?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not definitive, but there's a lot of evidence.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: So you claim. However, you never actually told me where this evidence came from.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It started with a parcel delivered to Phileas Rimor. It contained a journal written by King Shayzien VII himself. The journal mentioned creatures on Quidamortem.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: A parcel? King Shayzien VII's journal was delivered in a parcel? Who sent it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Well you need to find out. Go to Phileas Rimor and retrieve this parcel. I need to see it for myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eugh, so much walking. Fine, I'll go see Phileas.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, this doesn't look good. I'd better tell Lord Shayzien.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Do you have the parcel citizen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not. I went to see Phileas but his home was trashed and he was gone.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Gone? That's not good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: I'll open an investigation into this immediately. Hopefully we can find out what happened to Phileas.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about the research?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: I'm afraid that without that parcel we don't have anything to link King Shayzien VII to your discoveries.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: However, the rest of your research is still valid. I'll contact the research team on the mountain and make sure their discoveries are documented in the Arceuus Library.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything I can do?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: You've done enough for now citizen. I'll be in touch if we require your assistance again. For now, here is payment for your time.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Need anything else citizen?
